description: Sensitive metadata (author names, GPS coordinates, software versions) embedded in publicly accessible documents, images, or files.
detection: Scan public-facing files for embedded metadata using ExifTool or FOCA.
mitigation: Strip metadata before publishing. Use metadata scrubbers in CI/CD pipeline.

description: An entity (person, organization, or domain) repeatedly appears in data breach databases, indicating systemic security hygiene failures.
detection: Monitor breach notification services for mentions of organizational email domains.
mitigation: Enforce MFA, credential rotation policies, and monitor for credential stuffing attempts.

description: Aggregating public social media posts, connections, and activity patterns to profile individuals or organizations, revealing behavioral patterns, schedules, affiliations, and vulnerabilities.
detection: Monitor for unusual third-party API queries on social accounts.
mitigation: Audit public profile visibility settings. Train employees on OPSEC.

description: Systematically querying DNS records and brute-forcing subdomains to map an organization's full internet footprint, identifying internal systems, staging environments, and forgotten assets.
detection: Monitor CT logs for new certificate issuances on your domains.
mitigation: Disable zone transfers. Audit and shut down unused subdomains. Use split-horizon DNS.

description: Using credentials exposed in historical data breaches to attempt authentication against current services via credential stuffing or password spraying.
detection: Detect unusual login volumes and logins from new geographies or IPs.
mitigation: Enforce MFA everywhere. Block known breach passwords at login.

description: Analyzing job postings to infer an organization's technology stack, security gaps, planned projects, and internal tools from required skills and qualifications.
detection: Difficult to detect; this is passive OSINT by the adversary.
mitigation: Sanitize job postings to avoid exposing specific vendor names and internal tool stacks.

description: Scanning public code repositories (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) for accidentally committed secrets: API keys, passwords, database connection strings, private certificates.
detection: Use GitHub secret scanning alerts. Integrate Gitleaks or TruffleHog in CI/CD.
mitigation: Never commit secrets. Use environment variables + secret managers (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). Rotate any exposed credential immediately.

description: Determining precise physical locations of individuals or infrastructure by correlating public data: photo backgrounds, IP geolocation, social media check-ins, street view imagery.
detection: Monitor for mass lookups of organizational IP ranges.
mitigation: Strip GPS metadata from photos. Use privacy-preserving proxies for public-facing services.

description: Extracting domain registration data, registrant names, emails, and organization details from WHOIS databases and historical WHOIS archives to identify infrastructure ownership and pivot to related assets.
mitigation: Use domain privacy protection. Ensure registrant emails are not tied to operational accounts.

description: Using internet scanning platforms to identify publicly exposed services, open ports, misconfigured devices, and unpatched software versions belonging to a target organization.
detection: Monitor Shodan/Censys for your own IP ranges. Set up alerts for new exposures.
mitigation: Minimize internet-exposed attack surface. Implement firewall rules. Use vulnerability management tools.

pattern_name: Paste Site Monitoring for Sensitive Data
description: Monitoring paste sites (Pastebin, Ghostbin, Rentry) for leaked internal data, credentials, source code snippets, and internal communications posted by insiders or attackers post-compromise.
detection: Use PasteHunter or Dumpmon to continuously monitor for organizational keywords.
mitigation: Implement DLP controls. Monitor paste sites for organizational keywords and domains.

description: Building detailed profiles of employees including their roles, reporting structure, technology skills, project history, and connections to identify high-value targets for spearphishing or social engineering.
detection: Monitor for mass profile views on corporate LinkedIn accounts.
mitigation: Security awareness training on OPSEC. Limit public exposure of sensitive role details.

description: Using web archive services to access old versions of websites, retrieving previously exposed data, deprecated admin panels, old API endpoints, or removed sensitive content.
mitigation: Submit robots.txt disallow rules to archive services. Use legal takedown for sensitive cached content.

description: Monitoring public certificate transparency logs to discover new subdomains, internal project names, and infrastructure changes as TLS certificates are issued.
detection: Use CT log monitoring defensively to detect unauthorized certificate issuance for your domains.
mitigation: Monitor CT logs for your own domains. Use CAA DNS records to restrict which CAs can issue certificates.

description: Monitoring dark web forums, markets, and Telegram channels for mentions of target organizations, sale of stolen data, planned attacks, or recruitment of insiders.
detection: Use dark web monitoring services (Recorded Future, DarkOwl, Flare).
mitigation: Proactive dark web monitoring. Incident response plan for when data is found on dark web.
